title: cotyledon breviflora var. subbrachyantha
intro:
Cotyledon breviflora var. subbrachyantha, commonly known as the 'red-leaved pig's ear', is a succulent plant species in the Crassulaceae family, characterized by its distinctive red-edged leaves and unique growth habit.

This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties due to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with Cotyledon breviflora var. subbrachyantha include its potential to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, making it a promising candidate for the treatment of various health conditions. The plant's leaves can be used to prepare herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
